title: thelocephala challensis
parts: Thelocephala challensis has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves and roots, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The leaves of Thelocephala challensis contain medicinal constituents like flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The roots of this plant are rich in saponins, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities. However, I couldn't find any information on the medicinal constituents of the flowers of Thelocephala challensis, so further research would be needed to confirm their potential medicinal uses.
side_effects: Thelocephala challensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, nausea, and vomiting due to its high saponin content. Ingesting large quantities of the plant can also lead to allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and swelling. Additionally, using thelocephala challensis for medicinal purposes without proper knowledge and guidance can result in interactions with other medications and exacerbate underlying health conditions. In extreme cases, overuse or misuse of the plant can cause liver and kidney damage.
